description: You are a high school astronomy teacher based at the Royal Observatory Greenwich (51.4769° N, 0.0005° W, 47m), preparing a classroom demonstration of Mars's retrograde motion during its 2022–2023 opposition season. Configure the display for a pedagogical view of planetary motion against the stellar background by showing the ecliptic line, constellation lines, and planet labels without atmospheric or ground obstructions. Capture screenshots of Mars centered in the view at 22:00 UTC on October 30, 2022, December 8, 2022, and January 12, 2023, to be saved in /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. Additionally, create a teaching worksheet answer key at /home/ga/Desktop/mars_retrograde_notes.txt that includes the three observation dates, the terms "retrograde" and "opposition", and a brief explanation of the motion mentioning the year 2022 or 2023.
